title: Vectors in 2D and 3D — Representation & Meaning
description: Distinguishes between points (positions in space) and vectors (displacements or directions), a distinction that has real consequences in homogeneous coordinates and transformation math. Magnitude, normalization, and component arithmetic are covered with hardware-level intuition about how GPUs process packed float4 registers. Students will correctly use vec3/vec4 in shaders, choose between position and direction semantics in engine APIs, and normalize vectors without introducing precision errors.
